speaker: RAYA
text: I know what you’re thinking. A lone rider, a dystopian world, a land that’s gone to waste - how did this world get so broken? Well, that all began 500 years ago. The camera pulls high into the air, showing an aerial shot of Kumandra.

speaker: RAYA
text: Kumandra. This is what we used to be, when our land was whole and we lived harmoniously alongside dragons - magical creatures who brought us water and rain and peace. DRAGONS leap out of the river like dolphins.

speaker: RAYA
text: It was paradise. A beautiful shot of Kumandran people and dragons living harmoniously.

speaker: RAYA
text: But then, the druun came - a mindless plague that spread like wildfire - multiplying as they consumed life and turned everyone they touched into stone. The smoke-like DRUUN turns someone into stone by floating over them. As it does, the Druun multiplies into two. A war between the Druun and the Dragons ensue.

speaker: RAYA
text: The dragons fought for us the best they could, but it wasn’t enough. Druun turn dragons and people into stone by the dozens. Amid the chaos, we find SISU, a majestic blue Dragon who climbs to the highest point in Kumandra.

speaker: RAYA
text: That’s when the mighty Sisudatu, the last dragon, concentrated all her magic into a gem and... She centers all her magic and -

speaker: RAYA
text: ... blasted the druun away. A MAGICAL EXPLOSION WIPES AWAY THE DRUUN and returns everyone back to normal.

speaker: RAYA
text: Everyone that was turned to stone came back... except the dragons. We see a stone dragon graveyard.

speaker: RAYA
text: All that was left of Sisu was her gem. Where Sisu stood, there’s now a glowing Dragon Gem.

speaker: RAYA
text: It should have been this big inspirational moment where humanity united over her sacrifice... but instead - people being people - they all fought to possess the last remnant of dragon magic. We see the map of Kumandra split up.

speaker: RAYA
text: Borders were drawn. Kumandra divided. We all became enemies. And the gem had to be hidden. We see a palace erected around the gem.

speaker: RAYA
text: But that’s not how the world broke. That didn’t truly happen until 500 years later when I came into the story...

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Looks like someone’s trying to be clever. She reaches into her satchel and pulls out what looks like a SMALL ARMORED BALL. She taps on it. The ball opens, revealing THE CUTEST FURRY FACE YOU HAVE EVER SEEN. This is TUK-TUK, half pill-bug, half puppy.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Alright, Tuk-Tuk, let’s show ’em what clever really looks like. TUK-TUK turns back into a ball and rolls down the chamber hallway, triggering BOOBY TRAPS: swinging nets spring up, designed to trap intruders. But Tuk-Tuk rolls under the nets unscathed. Tuk-Tuk unfurls and walks down the hallway, but gets distracted by a passing BUG.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Tuk-Tuk! Come on. Focus. Tuk-Tuk obliges and stays on task.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Thank you. Raya crawls safely under the nets behind Tuk-Tuk.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Hey, bud. That was awesome. Gimme some shell. Tuk-Tuk tries to high-five her, but ends up rolling over onto his back.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: I gotcha. Raya comes to a large circular door and unlocks it.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Wait a second... this feels too easy... She turns to see A GOLDEN MASKED WARRIOR standing between her and the Gem.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Chief Benja. Look, I know it’s your job to try and stop me, but you won’t.

speaker: BENJA
text: Don’t mistake spirit for skill, young one. I promise you will not set foot on the Dragon Gem’s inner circle. Not even a toe.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: You might want to take out that blade. You’re gonna need it.

speaker: BENJA
text: Not today. The Masked Warrior detaches his sword from his belt, but doesn’t remove it from its sheathe. Raya attacks. THEY FIGHT - it’s fun, athletic. They’re both highly skilled, but Benja is clearly the superior fighter. He disarms her Raya and puts her on point. With the tip of his sheathed sword inches away from her face, he then...

speaker: BENJA
text: Like I said, not one foot on the inner circle. You lost, Raya.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Did I? The Masked Warrior looks down to see that Raya has her toe touching the Gem’s inner circle platform.

speaker: BENJA
text: Raya... ... I probably should have said two feet.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Hey. Don’t beat yourself up too much, Chief Benja. You gave it your best.

speaker: BENJA
text: I won’t. And it’s either father or ba to you. You did good, dewdrop. You passed the test. He gestures for her to step onto the Gem’s circle. Raya takes a breath and does - it’s a moment she’s been working towards her whole life. Raya is transfixed by the Gem’s brilliance. Her cockiness falls away as she’s caught up in its magic.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Wow. The spirit of Sisu. Benja smiles - he’s been waiting for this moment his whole life too. He approaches the Gem and kneels, Raya follows suit. She looks, entranced, at the GLOWING WATER DROPLETS spiraling around and above the Gem.

speaker: BENJA
text: For generations, our family has sworn to protect the gem. Today, you will join that legacy. Raya’s bows as Benja scoops up water with his hands and pours it on her. It’s a sacred, intimate ceremony. As the water trickles down her face, to Raya’s amazement the droplets start to GLOW and float all around her, joining the droplets encircling the Gem.

speaker: BENJA
text: Raya, Princess of Heart, my daughter. You are now a Guardian of the Dragon Gem. Benja looks at his daughter with pride, as Raya accepts this new responsibility. He puts an arm around her as she leans her head on his shoulder. This is everything she’s ever wanted. SEQ 126 - DAD’S WISDOM

speaker: BENJA
text: Well, someone’s excited.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Anyone hoping to steal the dragon gem now has to face the fury of the TWO baddest blades in all the lands.

speaker: BENJA
text: I’m glad you feel prepared, dewdrop, because I have something important to tell you - the other lands... they’re on their way here as we speak.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: They are? Benja nods.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Okay, OKAY, we can do this. I’m ready. I know exactly how we’ll stop them.

speaker: BENJA
text: Really? Tell me what you know about the other lands.

speaker: CATS
text: Hisss! The cat swipes at camera as...

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Okay, so we’re gonna need crossbows. And catapults. Ooo, what about flaming catapults? Benja walks over to a soup pot...

speaker: BENJA
text: Or... how about - Shrimp paste from Tail, lemongrass from Talon, Bamboo shoots from Spine, chilis from Fang, and palm sugar from Heart.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: We’ll poison them?

speaker: BENJA
text: No, we’re not going poison them and we’re not going to fight them. We’re going to share a meal with them.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Wait, what?

speaker: BENJA
text: I invited them. Benja hands her a bowl of soup.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: But they’re our enemies.

speaker: BENJA
text: They’re only our enemies because they think the dragon gem magically brings us prosperity.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: That’s ridiculous. It doesn’t do that.

speaker: BENJA
text: They assume it does just like we assume things about them. Raya, there’s a reason why each land is named after a part of the dragon. We were once unified harmoniously as one. Kumandra.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: That’s ancient history, ba.

speaker: BENJA
text: But it doesn’t have to be. Listen, if we don’t stop and learn to trust one another again, it’s only a matter of time before we tear each other apart. This isn’t the world I want you to live in. Raya nods.

speaker: BENJA
text: I believe that we can be Kumandra again. But someone has to take the first step. Trust me.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Thing’s look a little tense, ba.

speaker: BENJA
text: Don’t worry. I’m gonna open with a joke.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Please, don’t.

speaker: BENJA
text: I’m kidding, I’m kidding. Benja steps forward.

speaker: BENJA
text: People of Tail, Talon, Spine, and Fang, welcome to Heart. For far too long we have been enemies. But today is a new day. Today, we can be Kumandra once more. Benja gestures, inviting them to cross the bridge, but no one moves.

speaker: TAIL CHIEF
text: Nice speech, Chief Benja, but why’d you really bring us here? Are you gonna rob us?

speaker: DANG HAI
text: Why would he need to rob us? The land of Heart already has everything.

speaker: SPINE CHIEF
text: It’s easy to pontificate on Kumandra when you hold the mightiest weapon in all the lands.

speaker: BENJA
text: The gem’s not a weapon, it’s a sacred relic. Raya looks around and sees all the angry voices shouting from the crowds. Her eyes catch NAMAARI’s . They share a smile as all the adults argue away. Raya makes a decision and steps forward, gives a respectful bow before speaking.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: I have something to say... The crowds hushes...

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Who’s hungry? No one in the crowd responds... except for one hand raises, a LANKY SPINE WARRIOR from the congregation of Spine. His fellow warriors eye-shame him into lowering it. Namaari giggles at him. She looks up at her mother, VIRANA , asking for permission to approach.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Go ahead. It’s alright. Namaari walks to Raya.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: I’m Namaari. Of Fang.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Hi Namaari, I’m Raya. Raya notices Namaari’s necklace.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Is that Sisu?! Sorry. I might be a little bit of a dragon nerd.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Hey. I’m the one wearing the Sisu fan-necklace. The girls are clearly cut from the same cloth. Raya invites Namaari into the palace.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Come on. Have you eaten yet? The two girls follow each other over the bridge into the palace as Benja smiles, proud of his daughter. The sight of these two girls connecting lowers everyone’s defenses, and the rest of the clans, too, cross the bridge.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Seriously? Your mom actually said that? Awkward. Namaari laughs in acknowledgment.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Okay, next question - hand-to-hand or swords?

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Blades all day.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Right? Kumandran fist-bump.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Okay... dressy or casual?

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Only a monster would choose to wear this outfit on the regular. Raya looks around for inspiration for her next question, she sees their food.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Rice or stew? Namaari however goes silent.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: I didn’t think that’d stump you.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: This is actually one of the first times I’ve had rice in a while.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Really?

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Fang may look nice on the outside, but we have some pretty big holes on the inside.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Oh.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Sorry, didn’t mean to bring it down. So where were we? We both have single parents who are terrible at telling jokes, we’re both warrior-women who despise uncomfortable formal wear -

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: - AND we’re both Sisu super fans.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: You know, Fang legend says she’s still out there.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Sisu? You’re kidding, right?

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Wanna see something? Young Namaari looks around. No one is paying attention to them. She pulls out a scroll. She handles it gently with reverence.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Are you supposed to have that?

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: No. Namaari opens the scroll, revealing an intricate design of Sisu and the Druun.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: According to this, after the Mighty Sisu blasted away all the druun, she fell into the water and floated downstream. Legends say she’s now sleeping at the river’s end. Tuk Tuk crawls onto the scroll and looks at the design with the girls.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: But which river? There’s like hundreds.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: I don’t know. But if we could find it, could you imagine? A dragon back in the world? Things could be so much better.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Yeah, maybe we really could be Kumandra again. Namaari makes a decision. She takes off her necklace and hands it to Raya.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Here.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Whoa. Really?

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: From one dragon nerd to another. Raya looks at the pendant and back at Namaari, gets an idea.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Hey. Come with me, dep la. I wanna show you something. The girls stand to leave. Tuk-Tuk takes a bite of food, then follows Raya.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: The spirit of Sisu. I can feel it. Raya smiles, happy she has a pal to share this with.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: It’s the last bit of dragon magic left in the whole world.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: I see why Heart guards it so closely. Thank you, dep la. You’ve been very helpful. Namaari suddenly strikes Raya, sending her to the ground. She looks on in shock at Namaari.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: In a different world, maybe we coulda been friends. But I have to do what’s right for Fang. Namaari goes for the Gem, but Raya leaps in front of her. They start fighting. Seeing that Raya’s in trouble, Tuk-Tuk rolls in to help. He bumps into Namaari’s ankle, but she scoots him away. TukTuk shakes it off, angry. This distracts Namaari just long enough for Raya to strike, knocking her to the ground. However, Namaari isn’t worried at all. Instead she smiles and shoots a firework up through the temple opening.

speaker: BENJA
text: Chief Virana, I’m so glad Fang accepted our invitation. The firework explodes in the night sky. Everyone looks up. The congregation of Fang immediately begins moving out towards the Gem temple. Benja looks back to the Fang Queen to discover... she’s gone.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: There is no way you’re taking Sisu’s Gem. Namaari steps away from Raya towards the chamber doors.

speaker: YOUNG NAMAARI
text: Sorry. It’s Fang’s now. Suddenly, the room fills up with Fang soldiers. Raya steps back, she’s the only thing between Fang and the gem. Tuk-Tuk climbs up on Raya’s shoulder, frightened. Raya bravely squares up against the Fang forces, until - SWOOSH!!! Benja repels into the room from above and quickly dispatches a dozen different adversaries. His sword turns into a cool grappling whip which he uses to drive everyone back.

speaker: BENJA
text: You will not set foot on the Dragon Gem’s inner circle. A giant smile crosses Raya’s face. Her dad is indeed the baddest blade in all of Kumandra. But then...

speaker: FEMALE SPINE WARRIOR
text: What’s going on?

speaker: SPINE CHIEF
text: What is this? The other armies from Tail, Talon, and Spine begin to shuffle into the room. Suddenly they all stop and see the gem for the first time. They’re in awe.

speaker: FEMALE SPINE WARRIOR
text: Fang’s making a play for the Gem!

speaker: SPINE CHIEF
text: No! Spine should have the gem!

speaker: TAIL CHIEF
text: Not if we get to it first! Everyone pulls out their weapons. This is going to be a blood bath. Benja sees Raya in the reflection of his sword as she raises her fists to help defend the Gem. He makes a decision.

speaker: BENJA
text: Listen to me! Everyone has their weapons raised -

speaker: BENJA
text: We have a choice. We can tear each other apart, or we can come together and build a better world. It’s not too late. I still believe we can be Kumandra again... Benja sheathes his sword. Did it work? Suddenly, an arrow strikes Benja in the leg. As he sinks, everyone rushes in.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Ba! Raya runs to Benja’s side as... Hands grabs onto the gem, it gets tossed around until - KRSHH! The Gem crashes to the ground, breaking into five pieces. But before anyone can react - BOOM! The ground shakes, the water in the chamber recedes, the floor opens up. From the broken Earth, A DRUUN emerges.

speaker: BENJA
text: Druun... The ARCHER shoots an arrow at the creature, but it goes right through the Druun, leaving him unscathed. The Druun immediately turns the archer to stone, then duplicates. The Druun turns toward Benja and Raya. Benja quickly rushes to grab one of the gem pieces and holds it up... repelling the Druun back momentarily. There’s a mad scramble as the other four gem pieces get snatched up.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: No! Raya grabs her father, pulling him out of the fray.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Ba! Come on! We have to go! A Druun pursues after them, but touches water and recoils!

speaker: BENJA
text: They’re repelled by water. Hurry! Get to the river!

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Ba! Get up! Come on. Please, we have to keep moving. Get up. He looks ahead, the distance is too far for him to go. The bridge SHAKES again. Benja sees a Druun approaching.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Please, ba! We don’t have time. Look. Stand up! I’ll help you.

speaker: BENJA
text: Raya, you have to listen. You are the Guardian of the Dragon Gem.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Ba, why are you saying this? Benja pulls out the broken gem shard.

speaker: BENJA
text: There’s still light in this. There’s still hope.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: No, we can make it together. You’re okay...

speaker: BENJA
text: Raya, don’t give up on them. Benja hands Raya the gem. He pulls her in close, then kisses her on the forehead.

speaker: BENJA
text: I love you, my dewdrop.

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: Ba? Benja pushes Raya off the bridge into the water just as...

speaker: YOUNG RAYA
text: NO! ... a druun passes through her father, turning him to stone.. As the current carries her away, Raya watches her father’s unmoving silhouette on the bridge.

speaker: Chyron
text: TAIL - SIX YEARS LATER

speaker: RAYA
text: Please let this be it. She begins to drift out of frame.

speaker: RAYA
text: Whoa, what are you doing you big, fur bug? Hey buddy - focus. Eyes forward, Tuk-Tuk. Tuk-Tuk turns back to the cliffside.

speaker: RAYA
text: Good boy! You’re so easily distracted. Just then, a DRUUN attacks! Raya and Tuk-Tuk fall back. Raya scrambles for her satchel. Just as the Druun closes in, Raya pulls out her gem shard and holds it up, scaring the Druun off. She turns around to find Tuk-Tuk immobile on his back, looking at her helplessly.

speaker: RAYA
text: You’re getting a little too big for this, bud.

speaker: RAYA
text: Six years of searching and we end up at a literal shipwreck. That’s not a bad sign, is it? Raya approaches the wreck and enters it. Tuk-Tuk tries to follow but can’t fit into the opening.

speaker: TUK-TUK
text: Hrmph.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisudatu... I don’t know if you’re listening. I’ve searched every river to find you. And now I’m here at the very last one. Look, there’s not a lot of us left and we really... we really need your help. If I can be honest, I really need your help. I made a mistake. I trusted someone I shouldn’t have. And now the world’s broken. Sisudatu, I just really... really want my ba back. Please. She takes a deep breath.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay. Here goes everything. Raya raises a vial of water to her head and bows. She pours the water over the pendant in the bowl...

speaker: RAYA
text: Suva de dra sim Mandra de dra lim Bavaa de dra Tomben. She pours the last of the water from the bottle, and raises the bottle to her head again. Raya sits in anticipated silence, but nothing seems to happen. However, unbeknownst to her, the stream behind her begins to flow backwards. Water droplets rise out of the stream and swirl in the air. Tuk-Tuk sees this from the outside entrance, as he drinks from a puddle. Tuk-Tuk starts “barking” to get Raya’s attention.

speaker: RAYA
text: I know, buddy, I haven’t forgot - Raya now sees the floating water droplets which now are coming together to magically form... SISU!

speaker: SISU
text: Where am I? Pengu? Amba? Pranee? Are ya here? Seeing Sisu’s silhouette, Raya bows.

speaker: RAYA
text: Oh Mighty Sisu -

speaker: SISU
text: Who said that? Sisu turns, her mighty tail swings out and clobbers the bowing Raya. Sisu steps out of the dust-cloud. We finally see her in her mighty glory.

speaker: SISU
text: Hello? Hello?

speaker: RAYA
text: Mmf-mph!! Sisu looks down and finds Raya awkwardly collapsed in a heap under her, perhaps tangled up in her own cape.

speaker: SISU
text: Ooo, I’m sorry, I didn’t see ya there. Not too bad. Just a little dusty. Let me get that for you. Raya and Sisu finally make eye-contact.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu. You are... Sisu!

speaker: SISU
text: And you’re... people. What’s your name?

speaker: RAYA
text: Raya. I’m Raya.

speaker: SISU
text: And you’re NOT made of stone, which means... Both women squeal in celebration!

speaker: SISU
text: It worked!

speaker: RAYA
text: It worked!

speaker: SISU
text: We did it! Ya hear that, Pengu? It worked! I didn’t mess it up! Is that food? I was so focused on saving the world, I forgot to have breakfast today.

speaker: RAYA
text: Today? When exactly do you think “today” is?

speaker: SISU
text: Tuesday. Ugh! I mean... Mmm. What is this delightful culinary treat?

speaker: RAYA
text: It’s jackfruit jerky. I dried it myself.

speaker: SISU
text: Well, compliments to the chef. Wanna finish this, skippy? Sisu offers the jerky to Tuk-Tuk, but he refuses to eat it.

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh, Sisu, there are a few things I need to catch you up on...

speaker: SISU
text: Oh yeah?

speaker: SISU
text: You broke it!?! Birds fly.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh, oh, oh my. Oh, this is bad. This is bad. I’ve been asleep for 500 years, you brought back the druun, and none of my brothers and sisters came back? Why didn’t they come back?

speaker: RAYA
text: I... don’t know.

speaker: SISU
text: Also you broke the gem...

speaker: RAYA
text: I still have a big chunk of it though.

speaker: SISU
text: Is that supposed to make me feel better? If you lost a puppy and I said “Well we still have a big chunk of it,” would that make you feel better?

speaker: RAYA
text: Can’t you just make another one?

speaker: SISU
text: No, I can’t just make another one.

speaker: RAYA
text: But you’re a dragon.

speaker: SISU
text: I’m gonna be real with you, alright? I’m not like the best dragon. You know?

speaker: RAYA
text: But you saved the world.

speaker: SISU
text: I did do that. That’s true. But... have you ever done like a group project, but there’s like that one kid who didn’t pitch in as much but still ended up with the same grade? Yeah... I wasn’t the one who actually made the gem, I just... turned it in. Sisu picks up the gem shard and immediately starts glowing.

speaker: RAYA
text: Whoa, you’re glowing.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh, thank you, I use aloe and river slime to maintain my--

speaker: RAYA
text: No, no-- look. Sisu looks at herself.

speaker: SISU
text: This was my little sister Amba’s magic. I got the glow!

speaker: RAYA
text: Your little sister’s magic?

speaker: SISU
text: Yeah, every dragon has an unique magic.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, what’s yours?

speaker: SISU
text: I’m a really strong swimmer!

speaker: RAYA
text: Wait wait, you touched this gem piece and it gave you powers. You know what this means, right?

speaker: SISU
text: I no longer need a nightlight?

speaker: RAYA
text: What? No, you’re still connected to the gem’s magic. And that means you can still use it to save the world. If we get all the other gem pieces,

speaker: RAYA
text: You can reassemble it and -

speaker: SISU
text: I can reassemble it and - Sisu looks at the scroll, sees herself looking awesome.

speaker: RAYA
text: Boom the druun away!

speaker: SISU
text: Boom the druun away!

speaker: RAYA
text: And bring my ba back?

speaker: SISU
text: And bring ALL of Kumandra back.

speaker: WAHN
text: Princess Namaari, the Tail-lands are infested with Druun. Benja’s daughter is as good as stone out here. Retrieving some useless dragon scroll isn’t worth the risk. Namaari drops Wahn with a leg sweep.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Anyone else want to question why we’re out here? Assembled group is speechless. The Serlot army charges forward, following Tuk-Tuk’s tracks to the wrecked ship. It looks like Raya is gonna get caught, however... In the background, the silhouette of Raya and Sisu ride away on Tuk-Tuk in the distance.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, so here’s the sitch -

speaker: RAYA
text: After the gem broke, each piece was taken by one of the chiefs of the five lands. Fang - A SPLITSCREEN SHOWING THE FANG CHIEF HOLDING A GEM SHARD slams onto the far-left of the screen.

speaker: RAYA
text: Heart - ANOTHER SPLITSCREEN SHOWING RAYA HOLDING A SHARD NEXT TO TUK TUK slams in next.

speaker: RAYA
text: Spine -

speaker: RAYA
text: Talon - A SPLITSCREEN SHOWING DANG HAI WITH A GEM SHARD

speaker: RAYA
text: - and Tail... A FINAL SPITSCREEN SHOWING THE TAIL CHIEF WITH A GEM SHARD

speaker: RAYA
text: ... where we are now. Raya’s stands in front of an impressive structure that’s now the Tail Chief’s home. CU on Sisu’s face.

speaker: SISU
text: Wow, so many questions. First one - Why am I wearing this? Pull back to reveal Sisu dressed exactly like Raya.

speaker: RAYA
text: Well, we don’t want to attract attention. Raya and Sisu look up at the old Tail Ruins.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh, you definitely chose the right hat for that. So what makes you think the Tail Chief’s here? Sisu hits a trip wire, a spiky tree-trunk falls in her direction. Raya dives, pushes Sisu out of the way as the tree trunk crashes down where she once stood.

speaker: RAYA
text: Because this place didn’t just boobytrap itself.

speaker: SISU
text: This doesn’t make sense. None of this would stop a druun.

speaker: RAYA
text: It’s not to stop druun. It’s to stop people.

speaker: SISU
text: Hm... They look down hallway and see an intricate array of triplines. Raya carefully works her way through it. She looks back at Sisu who quickly and smoothly flies through the obstacle course without any problem. Sisu shoots Raya a smile. Raya’s blown away by what she’s just seen.

speaker: SISU
text: What? Why are you looking at me like that?

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh... nothing. Just not used to seeing dragons.

speaker: SISU
text: Impressed, huh? Wait till you see my backstroke. I’m wicked when I hit that liquid. I got water-skills that kill. I slaughter when I hit the water. I’m like... really good at swimming. Through rhyme. I was trying to make... that I was a really good swimmer... I’m a good swimmer... Is basically what I’m saying...

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, we need to keep going. Raya and Sisu round another dark corner.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh no!

speaker: RAYA
text: What? What is it?

speaker: SISU
text: We forgot to bring a gift for the Tail Chief.

speaker: RAYA
text: I’m sorry, a gift?

speaker: SISU
text: Yeah. A gift says “you can trust me, can I trust you?” A bug falls on Sisu’s nose.

speaker: SISU
text: Aw, hey there little fella! Oh, this beetle’s got a booty!

speaker: RAYA
text: Careful! It’s a Toot N Boom!

speaker: SISU
text: Why is it called a-- Bug farts... Raya tackles Sisu, as it explodes.

speaker: SISU
text: Got it. Noted. Makes sense. Raya looks up and sees a hallway full of bugs.

speaker: SISU
text: You have to admit though, these bug booties are kinda cute. They inch their way through.

speaker: RAYA
text: I’d say we found the Tail Chief.

speaker: SISU
text: What happened to her?

speaker: RAYA
text: From the looks of it, she was hoarding the gem and became a victim of her own traps.

speaker: SISU
text: Well, you got to admire her commitment. They look at the missing bridge.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, hold on. Sisu grabs Raya’s shoulders. Raya uses her sword to swing them over to the other side, but suddenly they stop mid-swing. They both look back and they see that’s Sisu’s hind-legs haven’t left the ledge.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh, we were doing a jumpy thing. So sorry. My bad. I get it now. I’m with it. Sisu jumps and propels them to the other side. They land on the other side. Sisu reaches for the gem.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu? Don’t. Raya points out the trip wire that runs from the skeleton to several pit-doors on the ceiling. Raya examines the booby-trap and does an “Indiana Jones” style switch off to get the gem without triggering the traps. It’s tense.

speaker: RAYA
text: Phew. Two down, three to go. The gem lands in Sisu’s hand and suddenly - poof - she’s a person.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: I just shape-changed! Into people!

speaker: RAYA
text: Dragons can do that?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: This was my sister Pranee’s thing! Sisu marches around weirdly.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Look at my people arms and my people face! Look at how close my butt is to my head. Now that you don’t have to hide me, getting the rest of the gems is gonna be a breeze!

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah well this one was easy, but the rest of them are being held by a bunch of no-good binturis.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Binturi? That’s not a very nice way to describe an old friend. Raya looks over to the entrance way and finds Namaari and her soldiers standing there.

speaker: RAYA
text: Namaari.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: What’s dripping, dep la? Oh, I see you finally made a new friend. And here I was worried you were gonna end up becoming a cat lady. Like me. Namaari’s Serlots stroll into the chamber.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Something tells me you’re not besties?

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Stealing dragon gem pieces, are we? Why?

speaker: RAYA
text: What can I say? Bling is my thing.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I gotta admit, Raya - until a few months ago I thought you were stone, but then someone stole Fang’s dragon scroll -

speaker: RAYA
text: Oh, is that why you’re chasing me? And here I thought it was because you missed me. Raya throws back Namaari her dragon scroll. Namaari catches it, face still looks severe.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Are you really looking for Sisu? What are you? Twelve?

speaker: RAYA
text: I actually was looking for Sisu. Ooh, and guess what? I found her. Sisu looks at Raya, are you serious? Namaari can’t hide the fact that she’s moved. Really?

speaker: RAYA
text: Say hi, Sisu!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: HI! It’s very nice to meet you. And I love your hair - and your cats’... hair.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Take them. Namaari’s people raise their crossbows. Raya quickly reacts, kicks the trip wire. Traps go off. The room begins to fill with sand. A ton of it dumps directly on Namaari and her feline army. Raya scoops up Sisu and swings her over to the other side.

speaker: RAYA
text: Run! Sisu tries to run, but with four legs.

speaker: RAYA
text: Not like that. Two legs!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Right! Raya and Sisu run past Namaari and her people. Namaari and her people unbury themselves and start chasing them. Raya looks forward, sees the Toot N Boom alley and begins agitating them to make them explode.

speaker: RAYA
text: Tuk Tuk, roll!

speaker: RAYA
text: Jump! They both jump off of Tuk Tuk, narrowly dodge the rock, and land back on Tuk Tuk.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Who was that girl?

speaker: RAYA
text: That’s Namaari. She’s the backstabbing binturi that broke the world Sisu looks back.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Wow, those cats are really fast, huh? Raya looks back and sees that Namaari and her soldiers are all now chasing them with their Serlots. Raya looks ahead and notices a boat docked in a port. An idea crosses her mind.

speaker: RAYA
text: What do cats and Druun have in common?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Um... they have no... souls!

speaker: RAYA
text: AND they both hate water.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Oh...

speaker: RAYA
text: Hold on! They leap into the water. Frustrated at being outmaneuvered. Namaari reroutes her soldiers in pursuit. SEQ. 225 - BOUN’S SHRIMP BOAT

speaker: RAYA
text: Hello?! Is anybody here? BOUN suddenly pops up behind Raya and Sisu, shoves chairs under them, throws a pop-up table in front of them, and slides up with a napkin over his fore-arm.

speaker: BOUN
text: Welcome to the world famous Shrimporium. My name is Boun, I’ll be your server today. Would you like to hear our daily specials?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Yes please!

speaker: BOUN
text: We got shrimp. We got congee. We got a shrimp congee that won’t quit.

speaker: RAYA
text: The captain! Where is the captain?

speaker: BOUN
text: Lemme go get him! What’s up, my new customers, I’m Captain Boun, the owner, chef, and Chief Financial Officer of the Shrimp-orium. How can I help you? Boun starts dancing.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Well, I’m Sisu and - Raya looks over the side of the ship, sees Serlots nearing.

speaker: RAYA
text: And we need to get to Talon. NOW.

speaker: BOUN
text: I’m sorry, the Shrimp-orium is not a water-taxi. Raya holds out blocks of jade in front of Boun, who nods.

speaker: BOUN
text: Toi! That’s alotta jade! Raya pushes half of it his way.

speaker: RAYA
text: Half now, half when we arrive in Talon. Deal? He grabs the jade.

speaker: BOUN
text: Clasp onto your congee, today’s special is-- To-go. Tuk-Tuk is about to take a bite when his food is yanked away. With a great flourish, Boun pushes the boat away with a long pole. But it floats at a snail’s pace. Raya sees Namaari and her Serlots in the distance, approaching fast.

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh... Captain Boun? Does this thing go any faster?

speaker: BOUN
text: Whoa, you didn’t tell me Fang was after you! This is gonna cost you extra!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Don’t worry, I got it! Sisu leaps into the water, and transforms back into a dragon.

speaker: BOUN
text: What is she doing? SISU grabs the bottom of the boat and starts kicking her legs. As she does, the shrimp-ship starts moving down the river at a steady speed.

speaker: BOUN
text: What is happening?

speaker: RAYA
text: My friend’s a really strong swimmer? Namaari and her Serlots reach the riverbank. They watch as the boat motors away.

speaker: RAYA
text: Bye bye, binturi! Namaari seethes.

speaker: FANG OFFICER
text: Princess Namaari. If they’re after gem pieces, their next stop would be Talon.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: We’re not following them to Talon. We’re going back to Fang. I need to speak with Chief Virana.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu? Sisu?? Sisu resurfaces.

speaker: SISU
text: Ah...

speaker: RAYA
text: Please! Get out of there!

speaker: SISU
text: I’m a water dragon. This is water. It’s sorta my thing. You wanna come in?

speaker: RAYA
text: Someone could see you.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh. Do you mean “Captain Pop and Lock” over there? What, are you scared he’s gonna challenge me to a dance battle? Boun bangs on the pots and pans to make a beat. Raya awkwardly dances to cover her talking to Sisu off the side of the ship.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu, I saw people lose their minds over a dragon gem. Can you imagine what they’d do over an actual dragon? Look, we need you to make this all work.

speaker: RAYA
text: Until we have all the gems, you have to stay human. Please.

speaker: SISU
text: Wow. You’ve really got some trust issues.

speaker: RAYA
text: Look, my father blindly trusted people and now he’s stone. Sisu jumps onto the boat as a human.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Hey. We’ll get your ba back. I got you, girl. Come on. Who’s your dragon? I mean human. Because I’m gonna be human until - yeah, you get it. Bowls suddenly fall onto a table.

speaker: BOUN
text: Okay, who’s hungry?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Ooo, I am! Boun ladles congee into each bowl.

speaker: BOUN
text: Two house specials! How spicy would you like it? Hot, hotter, or Boun-goes-the-dynamite?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Bring on the heat!

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, no, no... I don’t think so.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: What are you doing?

speaker: RAYA
text: We don’t know him. It could be poisoned.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Why would he poison us?

speaker: BOUN
text: Yeah, why would I poison you??

speaker: RAYA
text: First, to get my jade purse. Second, to steal my sword. And third - I don’t know - to kidnap my Tuk-Tuk.

speaker: BOUN
text: All good points, but if this is poison... You’re gonna die happy.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, thanks but, we’ve got our own eats. Raya takes out her own jackfruit jerky and rips a piece off with her teeth. It’s hard, leathery, not very good. Raya hands some to Sisu but finds her gulping down the congee.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: This is delicious! By the way. Not. Poison. But... It’s hot - IT’S REALLY HOT! Water! Boun? Captain Boun? We need water on deck!

speaker: RAYA
text: I know buddy.

speaker: BOUN
text: You know, during the day, you can almost forget they’re here, but at night... This is why I never leave the boat. Raya clocks Boun’s words. He might be verbose, but he’s just a kid.

speaker: RAYA
text: You’re a smart kid.

speaker: BOUN
text: What are druun anyways?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: A plague born from human discord, they’ve always been here - waiting for a moment of weakness to attack. They’re like the opposite of dragons, instead of bringing water and life to the world, they’re like a relentless fire that consumes everything in its wake until there’s nothing left except ash and stone.

speaker: BOUN
text: They took my family. Sisu takes flowers, walks to the edge of the boat, and drops petals into the river.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: They took mine too. Boun joins Sisu, takes some flowers and does the same. Raya clocks Boun’s sadness. She walks over, joins and casts one single flower into the river for her dad. It’s a solemn yet unifying moment. The lights from Talon catches Boun’s attention.

speaker: BOUN
text: Looks like we’re here! So where are you guys headed after Talon? I might be headed there too.

speaker: Chyron
text: TALON

speaker: BOUN
text: I mean, for a fee, of course.

speaker: RAYA
text: Of course. Talon, a bustling port city built almost entirely on the water. Protected from Druun by a WALL on one end, and water on the other - Talon is one of the very few towns left in the land. It has the looks of a Southeast Asian Night Market and the energy of New York’s night life... if Manhattan existed on sampan boats, that is.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Wow. What a smart way to druunproof your house - build right on the water. People of Talon are geniuses.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, Talon may look nice... but it’s a hotspot for pickpockets and con-artists.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Lucky for me, empty pockets!

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, so here’s the good news. I know where the gem piece is. The bad news...

speaker: RAYA
text: - it’s being held by the notorious Chief of Talon, Dang Hai. The camera lands on DANG HAI, an extra-large kingpin with a Cthulu-shaped beard.

speaker: RAYA
text: What Dang Hai lacks in style, he makes up in mean.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Gotcha. Now we’re just gonna have to turn up the charm. Let’s go get him a gift!

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu, I think maybe it’s safer for you to stay here on the boat.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: What?

speaker: RAYA
text: Without you we can’t put the gem back together.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: But I want to help.

speaker: RAYA
text: I know, and you will. By staying safe. Sisu reacts.

speaker: RAYA
text: I’ll be back before you know it. Raya leaves. Off Sisu, frustrated.

speaker: BOUN
text: If you see any hungry faces, send ’em my way.

speaker: RAYA
text: You got it, Captain. Raya peers towards the city.

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, there’s Dang Hai’s house. No detours until we score that dragon gem. Raya’s eyes are focused on the top of the Talon Chief’s home. Her ears however hear a BABY CRY. Raya turns and finds...

speaker: RAYA
text: Whoa. What in the - ... a lone baby sobbing in the middle of an alleyway. Raya looks up at the temple, she’s tempted to keep going, but her human decency gets the best of her. She approaches the toddler.

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh...okay... Raya picks up the crying baby.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey... baby, toddler, thing, whatever you’re called. It’s really late, what are you doing out here? Where are your parents? As Raya tries to console the baby, she turns to find...

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey. Whose baby - ... the Ongis on top of Tuk-Tuk, pick-pocketing the gems. They have the gem shards in their hands.

speaker: RAYA
text: What? Ongis! Drop em! Caught, the baby HISSES, attacks Raya, and runs off with the Ongis.

speaker: RAYA
text: Really? A con-baby!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: This is giving me bad feels. Chief Dang Hai does not sound like someone you can smooth talk. Raya didn’t even bring a gift. That’s it. I’m going shopping! Sisu starts marching off, then stops.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Oh wait... I don’t have any money.

speaker: BOUN
text: You’re an adult. You could just put it on credit.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Ooo, what’s credit?

speaker: BOUN
text: It’s kinda like a promise. You take what you want now and you promise you’ll pay it back later.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Pay it back later... What an amazing concept! Thanks, Captain Boun! A MUSICAL BEAT drops as...

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: I’ll be buying this with credit. Ooo, Dang Hai’s gonna love this. Credit, please. Oh, I like that. Uh, yeah. I’ll be using credit. Yes. I love credit! Sisu’s having a great time until -

speaker: MALE MERCHANT 1
text: HEY! YOU!

speaker: MALE MERCHANT 2
text: Are you going to pay for any of that?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Oh right, right. No, this is on credit. Yeah, I’m gonna pay you back later.

speaker: MALE MERCHANT 2
text: Pay us back later? We don’t know you!

speaker: FEMALE MERCHANT
text: You have no credit here! Pay us right now! The different Vendors circle around her.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Now? I don’t have anything, but... if I could find my girl Raya, she has a sword. Dried eats. Two Dragon Gem pieces. No we don’t. Whaaat? Who said that?

speaker: MALE MERCHANT 2
text: You better pay up, binturi!

speaker: FEMALE MERCHANT
text: This isn’t a charity!

speaker: MALE MERCHANT 2
text: Who do you think you’re stealing from? As the angry vendors close in on a beleaguered Sisu, a SWEET OLD WOMAN notices and comes to Sisu’s rescue.

speaker: DANG HU
text: Nee, nee! Get away from her! Can’t you see she’s new in town? The merchants back off, seemingly chastised. Sisu looks at the Old Lady, grateful. This is DANG HU.

speaker: DANG HU
text: Come, dear. Don’t be afraid. It’s okay. As Sisu walks off with Dang Hu.

speaker: RAYA
text: Ah! Diaper! ... and finally Tuk Tuk corners the Ongis and the Con-Baby. Having no escape, the little “Con-Baby” snaps her fingers and the Ongis hand Raya back her gem.

speaker: RAYA
text: Thanks.

speaker: RAYA
text: So, it’s none of my business, but using your baby charm to rip people off is super sketchy. Alright, where’s your family? Noi scoffs at Raya and walks away with the two largest Ongis. The LITTLEST ONGI however hangs back and “poses as a stone statue” to tell Raya what’s happened to Noi’s parents.

speaker: RAYA
text: Oh. Right. Raya looks over at Noi and the Ongis as they split a piece of food. She looks up at Dang Hai’s house and gets an idea.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey. How would you like to earn some honest loot?

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, Dang Hai, I’ll take that dragon gem piece. He turns to reveal -

speaker: CHAI THE FLOWER GUY
text: Whoa! I’m not Dang Hai! I’m Chai, the flower guy.

speaker: RAYA
text: Where is Dang Hai?

speaker: CHAI THE FLOWER GUY
text: He’s right over there. The Big Bodyguard points to a decorated statue... it’s a stoned Dang Hai.

speaker: RAYA
text: What? Who has his gem piece?

speaker: CHAI THE FLOWER GUY
text: The most vicious Chief Talon has ever seen.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Those folks were like crazy mad. I just wanted to bring some gifts to the Talon Chief, Dang Hai.

speaker: DANG HU
text: Is that who you’re looking for dear? I know exactly where he is.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: You do?

speaker: DANG HU
text: Mm-hmm. I’ll take you there.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: SEE! That’s what I’ve been trying to tell my girl Raya. But she’s all like - “You can’t trust people. Don’t talk to anyone. I only eat terrible foods I dried myself.” Two large HENCHMEN join Sisu and Dang-Hu as they walk. She gives them big smiles, but they don’t react to her at all. From the looks exchanged between Dang Hu and her “sons” we sense that she’s more Mob Boss than Sweet Old Mother. The Henchmen nod and lower a draw-bridge for Sisu and Dang Hu to cross. They cross the bridge and...

speaker: DANG HU
text: Come, dear. Chief Dang Hai is just outside the city, beyond the gates. ... they continue on through some gates.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Away from the water, huh? Wow. He really likes living on the edge.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Hi there, Dang Hai. I’m Sisu and I’ve come here to offer you some - - but is shocked to see it’s a Stone Person. The rattle of druun.... Sisu looks and finds a Druun emerge from the woods, it approaches Sisu - but a “glow” pushes it away. Dang Hu stands by the doors with her two large bodyguards...

speaker: DANG HU
text: Now, you’re going to tell me where I can find those other dragon gem pieces. Or... Dang Hu reveals she’s hold a dragon gem piece of her own.

speaker: DANG HU
text: I’ll have to leave you outside with that... thing. Take your pick. The Druun paces back and forth, looking for a way past Dang Hu. Dang Hu steps backward into the city walls, not giving Sisu a moment to react.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: But I trusted you.

speaker: DANG HU
text: Big mistake. Dang Hu steps behind the gates.

speaker: DANG HU
text: You better talk fast. It looks hungry. More druun begin to emerge from the woods.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: No, no, no. Mob Boss lowers the gem and the Druun rushes towards Sisu as - Raya and Tuk-Tuk blast through the city gates and grab-up Sisu -

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: RAYA!

speaker: DANG HU
text: Stop her!

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu, I told you to stay on the boat!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Sorry! As they sweep past Dang Hu, Raya swipes the gem from Dang Hu hands and gives it to Sisu.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hold onto this for me, will ya? Raya barrels towards them. A giant blast of FOG covers the area, scaring away the druun. Raya clears out. Door closes.

speaker: RAYA
text: Fog?!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Yeah, that was my brother Jagan’s magic. SEQ. 280 - WHERE’S THE BOAT?

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, three down, two to go! They hop on board.

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, Captain Boun. Next stop - Spine!

speaker: BOUN
text: Thanks for the new customers!

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, I sorta promised to buy them all the congee they could eat.

speaker: BOUN
text: Well, we’re stuck with them for awhile. Cause Ongis have nine stomachs.

speaker: RAYA
text: Oh, toi. Raya and Sisu walk over to the table.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: I can’t believe it. That old lady was really going to hurt me.

speaker: RAYA
text: Well, I’m sorry, Sisu, that’s what the world is now. You can’t trust anyone. As Sisu sits, Noi approaches her. She super adorable.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Does that include babies?

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh, well...

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: She’s so cute. I mean looks at those cheeks! Noi grabs Sisu by the cheeks.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Hi, I’m Sisu.

speaker: NOI
text: Soo-soo. Noi leaps on her face.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Aw, she loves my face.

speaker: RAYA
text: Watch out she doesn’t steal your teeth. Here, lemme help you.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Oh, that’s so sweet... okay, too much sweetness, too much sweetness! It’s too much!

speaker: VIRANA
text: And how do you propose we handle the druun, General Atitāya? Without proper protection, it would be a death sentence for our people. Namaari enters the palace.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I might have a solution for that, mother.

speaker: VIRANA
text: My little morning mist, it’s good to see you home.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I located Raya. She’s out stealing gem pieces.

speaker: VIRANA
text: What? Virana reacts, looks at her gem on a staff.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I’d like to take the royal army and intercept her in Spine.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Well, if she’s going into Spine, I doubt there will be much left of her to intercept.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: She’s more capable than you realize. We have to stop her. Virana hears the anger in her daughter.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Walk with me. Virana leads Namaari to the balcony.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Look around. We made all this by making smart decisions, not emotional ones. We are safe. Our canal protects us from those monsters. I don’t think it’s wise to risk yourself when you don’t have to.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: But you heard the General - we’re running out of space, we need to expand. If we had all the gem pieces, we could do that safely. You’re right.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: This isn’t an emotional decision, it’s the only decision we can make to secure Fang’s future. Virana thinks it over.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Namaari, you’re truly growing into the leader I raised you to be. General Atitāya, ready the royal army for my daughter’s command.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Thank you, Mother. I won’t let you down.

speaker: BOUN
text: Hey! Stop eating! The Ongis knock hot sauce into Tuk-Tuk’s plate. He eats and immediately knocks Raya over to get to the side of the ship.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sorry, Buddy. I got this. Raya walks over to Boun, Noi, and the Ongis fighting with each other.

speaker: BOUN
text: Did you just throw a shrimp at me? That’s not even edible! Don’t look at me like that, you fuzzy garbage can.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey, guys. Do you want to play hide and seek?

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Tuk-Tuk, Noi and the Ongis nod yes.

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, ready? One, two... They run away and hide. Raya stops counting and walks away.

speaker: BOUN
text: Thanks.

speaker: RAYA
text: Two and a half... Three... Remind me to never have kids.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Being people is hard.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yep. Six...Seven...

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: You have small heads, no tails, you lie to get what you want. Like the Talon Chief back there.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah well, the world’s broken, you can’t trust anyone.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Or maybe the world’s broken BECAUSE you don’t trust anyone.

speaker: RAYA
text: You sound just like my ba.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Well, he sounds like a smart man.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah. He was. I really wanted to believe him. I really wanted to believe that we could be Kumandra again.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: And we can.

speaker: RAYA
text: Literally thousands of people turned to stone would argue otherwise.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: That doesn’t mean you shouldn’t try.

speaker: RAYA
text: And I did. And you know what happened? I got kicked in the back by someone who gave me a “gift” - Look around. We’re a world of orphans because people couldn’t stop fighting over a gem. Wanna know why other dragons didn’t come back? Because people don’t deserve them.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: But you can change that.

speaker: RAYA
text: I am done trying. Kumandra is a fairy tale. The only thing important to me now is bringing my ba back. The ship comes to a stop.

speaker: BOUN
text: Um... I think we’re in Spine. Clouds part, revealing the imposing structure of Spine. Suddenly - Splash - Sisu pops out of the ship, storms up the beach-side. In her arms, she carries a large pot of Boun’s soup.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu!!!

speaker: BOUN
text: Hey! My congee!

speaker: RAYA
text: Don’t go anywhere. I’ll be right back. Raya leaps off the ship with her gem satchel and sword.

speaker: Chyron
text: SPINE

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu! Come back! Please. What are you doing?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: I’m going to show you that you’re wrong!

speaker: RAYA
text: How? By getting squashed by a bunch of Spine rage-heads?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: No. By proving to you that if you want to get someone’s trust, you have to give a little trust first. Sisu walks right up to the Spine village doors and starts knocking.

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu, don’t! Suddenly, a burlap sack swoops from above and traps Raya and Sisu inside.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: In hindsight, maybe I was a little hasty. But... who’s hungry? No? I’ll leave you alone.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay... Where are we?

speaker: SISU
text: Interesting choice of digs. Suddenly the doors of the cabin open. The silhouette of a large barbarian stands there, axe in hand. Tong slams his axe into the wall.

speaker: TONG
text: You two must be dung of brain to think you could steal Spine’s dragon gem. He slams his axe into some wood.

speaker: RAYA
text: Gem? Who said anything about gems? We have no interest in gems. Tong drops Raya’s satchel full of gems in it.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, yeah. I can see how that makes me look like a liar.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Actually, I think it was the lying that made you look like a liar. Tong starts maniacally laughing.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: I’m not sure what’s funny.

speaker: TONG
text: Your fear is like a delectable nectar feeding the tum tum of my soul. It’s good. It tastes like... mango.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Ooo, I love mango.

speaker: TONG
text: OF COURSE YOU LOVE MANGO! Only a tongue-less cretin wouldn’t.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Oh toi.

speaker: TONG
text: It’s been such a long time since I’ve last peered into the eyes of a trembling enemy.

speaker: TONG
text: Where has the time gone? It’s been so long... Tong slumps.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: He seems lonely.

speaker: TONG
text: NO! I’m not lonely. I’m a Spine warrior, I was born and bred to do only one thing - to invoke fear and to crush the skulls of my enemies.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: That’s actually two things.

speaker: TONG
text: RWAR!!!

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Ah!

speaker: TONG
text: Hehe. Look at your face.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey. What do you plan on doing with us?

speaker: TONG
text: Oh, it’s going to be bad. Horrifying. It’ll take me two weeks to clean up.

speaker: RAYA
text: You have no idea, do you?

speaker: TONG
text: Yes I do! I’m formulating this gruesome plan in my head of... It makes me sick just thinking about it. You just wait. Until then... ... why don’t you just hang around? Good one, huh? Hehe. Suddenly Tuk Tuk smashes through the front door as - Boun, Noi, and the Ongis leap off of him onto Tong. Tong is immediately subdued, gets tied up. Noi hisses.

speaker: TONG
text: A little one?

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: Alright! Boun cuts Raya and Sisu free.

speaker: RAYA
text: Good work, Captain Boun. Boun however is concerned about something else.

speaker: BOUN
text: Fang’s here.

speaker: RAYA
text: What?

speaker: NAMAARI
text: People of Spine -

speaker: NAMAARI
text: We are hunting for Raya, a fugitive from Heart. Send her out or we’re coming in!

speaker: RAYA
text: You’re the only one here?

speaker: TONG
text: My people battled the druun with much valor... but lost. Raya looks at Sisu and the bag of gem pieces. She realizes what’s at stake, makes a decision.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, the Fang gang’s here for me, not for you. So if I can distract them, you guys can get out of here.

speaker: SISU-HUMAN
text: You’re gonna fight an entire army?

speaker: RAYA
text: No, I’m just gonna stall them. Look, I know how to push Namaari’s buttons. Once you guys are clear, I’m out of there. What’s your name?

speaker: TONG
text: The moniker given to me is Tong.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, Tong, look, you don’t know me, I don’t know you. But I’m sure that you know a back door or way outta here and it’s really important that my friends stay safe. Okay? So, I am sincerely asking you, will you help us? Please. He nods.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Burn them out. The soldiers aim their flaming cross-bows. However... The giant gates of Spine open. All the Fang Soldiers lower their arrows as... Raya, alone, armed with only her sword, walks out.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hey there, princess undercut, fancy meeting you here.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: You and those dragon gem pieces are coming with me.

speaker: RAYA
text: My sword here says we’re not. All the Fang soldiers raise their weapons.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, I knew you couldn’t handle rolling solo. You’re nothing without your band. They start to advance. Did Raya’s bluff not work? Namaari’s hand finally signals her men to stop.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Stand down. This shouldn’t take long. Raya smiles. She got she wanted. The two women go at it. It’s a martial arts fantasia, as our Luke Skywalker battles with our movie’s Darth Vader. With everyone watching the fight, Raya is able to clock Tong, Sisu, and the kids start to escape. Raya goes back to the fight and disarms Namaari.

speaker: RAYA
text: Didja need that, dep la? Namaari kicks Raya’s sword out of her hands.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Nah. This is an evenly matched fight.

speaker: RAYA
text: Looks like somebody’s been taking classes. Raya charges and throws her signature move . ... but Namaari catches it in mid-air. Namaari smirks and lets loose a flurry of blows. She finally lands a hit.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Why are you stealing gem pieces? Raya clocks Tong and the gang escaping from the cabin.

speaker: RAYA
text: Oh, I’m just trying to get a matching set. Namaari knocks Raya down again...

speaker: RAYA
text: You didn’t happen to bring Fang’s gem, did you? Smack! Raya goes down again. Tong and company escape during the fight, but Sisu stops and looks back. She sees Raya on the ground, losing the fight.

speaker: RAYA
text: No? Never mind, I’ll just swing by and grab it later. That’s enough - Namaari reels back for the final blow.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Oh, I’m going to enjoy this... But she is interrupted by a blast of FOG. Sisu - in dragon form - scares away the other Fang soldiers. She gets in Namaari’s face and roars! Tong, Boun, the Baby and Ongis run up and also see the dragon. It stops them in their tracks, in awe. Raya gets up and runs to them.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yep. She’s a dragon. Let’s go. Come on! Raya ushers them along, as they continue to look back in shock. All of the Fang soldiers are reeling... But Namaari doesn’t flinch, staring in wonder at Sisu. Sisu locks eyes with Namaari, it rattles her. But then, just as fast as she arrived, Sisu turns and disappears into the safety of the forest. Off Namaari, heightened, emotional, in disbelief of what she just saw.

speaker: RAYA
text: Did you just hit me with a shrimp tail?? An angry Boun with Noi at his side chomping on shrimp.

speaker: BOUN
text: When were you going to tell us she was Sisu?

speaker: RAYA
text: Technically, you always knew she was Sisu. Another shrimp tail hits Raya.

speaker: RAYA
text: Seriously? Sisu climbs onboard. Tong steps forward.

speaker: TONG
text: Why are you here, divine water dragon?

speaker: SISU
text: Isn’t that obvious, big guy? My girl Raya and I are gonna fix the world - bring everyone back.

speaker: BOUN
text: You’re gonna bring everyone back? I want to help.

speaker: RAYA
text: I’m sorry, I can’t let you do that. It’s too dangerous.

speaker: BOUN
text: You’re not the only one who lost family to the Druun. Please let me help you. Noi and the Ongis kneel as well. Tong kneels and presents the Spine Dragon gem.

speaker: TONG
text: I too wish to join this fellowship of druun butt-kickery. Raya takes the gem and looks at Sisu. Sisu reaches out and touches the gem, her eyes brighten. Above them, water begins to fall.

speaker: SISU
text: My big brother Pengu’s magic... The team takes in the moment when...

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, Captain Boun. To Fang!

speaker: BOUN
text: You got it! MONTAGE: The boat gently drifts through the different landscapes of Kumandra - from temple ruins to marshlands.

speaker: RAYA
text: Alright, everyone. Here’s the plan. Spy music...

speaker: RAYA
text: The last gem piece is in Fang - - the most heavily guarded of the five lands.

speaker: RAYA
text: Now, they’re protected by an artificial canal that separates them from the rest of the world. The only way in or out is by water. The camera zooms into one of the ports and we see...

speaker: RAYA
text: Luckily for us, we have a magic water-dragon. ... Raya, Tong, Boun, Noi the Ongis, and Sisu-Human emerge from the water - all dressed as Fang citizens. The team strolls into the city, undetected. They approach the palace.

speaker: RAYA
text: Now, the palace will be swarming with Fang soldiers. Zoom into a group of Fang Soldiers, staring ruthlessly directly at the camera.

speaker: RAYA
text: To sneak past them, we’ll need to-- Boun steps forward.

speaker: BOUN
text: I got this, guys. The dozens of Fang soldiers all turn to Boun.

speaker: BOUN
text: I’ll take care of the first wave. Boun takes out guards with an impressive array of martial arts.

speaker: BOUN
text: Tong will follow up with his GIANT AXE OF BAD-AXERY! Tong slams his axe down in a fiery rage!

speaker: BOUN
text: And then come the Ongis and that crazy con-baby... They lunge at Virana, taking the gem.

speaker: BOUN
text: ... who will toss the gem to THE MIGHTY SISUDATU... RAWWWRRR...and then... Noi and the Ongis throw the last gem to Sisu.

speaker: SISU
text: Bye, bye, binturis - A bright light bursts out of the gem, making the screen go white.

speaker: BOUN
text: Super flow plan, am I right?

speaker: RAYA
text: Uh, no. Yeah, that’s not flow. That’s a clog.

speaker: SISU
text: I agree. Here’s my plan!

speaker: SISU
text: We infiltrate Fang, confront Namaari... Buff Sisu walks up to Namaari...

speaker: SISU
text: ... and offer her something nice and go... ... and gives her a present.

speaker: SISU
text: Hey. Want to help us save the world? Because all it takes is one gem piece!

speaker: NAMAARI
text: YES!!! I’ve been waiting for someone to ask me! Here ya go! Sisu and Namaari skip off into the sunset. NAMAARI Best friends forever!

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, I think I’d rather go with Boun’s plan.

speaker: SISU
text: What?

speaker: BOUN
text: YES!

speaker: SISU
text: Why?

speaker: RAYA
text: Because it’s Fang.

speaker: TONG
text: Their blades are specially designed for the stabbing of backs.

speaker: BOUN
text: If it weren’t for them, none of this would have happened. They’re the worst. Noi hisses.

speaker: SISU
text: If we’re just honest with her, deep down, I got a feeling she wants to fix the world as much as we do.

speaker: RAYA
text: You weren’t there when Namaari betrayed me. We’re sticking with my plan. Sisu scowls. More rain starts falling hard.

speaker: RAYA
text: Whoa! Uh, what’s with the downpour?

speaker: SISU
text: Come on. I need to show you something. Sisu grabs Raya and leaps off the boat into the water and runs on rain into the heavens. Boun, Tong, and Noi watch them fly away.

speaker: BOUN
text: So... what do we do now? Tong shrugs.

speaker: RAYA
text: Where are you taking me? The cloud parts to see where Sisu is headed: Heart. Raya’s eyes lock onto the Heart bridge. Sisu flies them into the top of the temple opening.

speaker: Chyron
text: Heart

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu, why did you bring me here?

speaker: SISU
text: This is where it all happened.

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah, I know. I was there.

speaker: SISU
text: No, this is where it all happened 500 years ago. Sisu pulls down some foliage to reveal the dragon statues.

speaker: SISU
text: I want you to meet my brothers and sisters. The real mighty ones. I miss them. Raya looks at the statues.

speaker: RAYA
text: I never knew they were here.

speaker: SISU
text: See that classy looking one over there? That’s Amba - I get my glow from her. And that’s Pranee - she’s the shapeshifter. Jagan - fog. And Pengu... he’s our big brother. He brings the rain. We were the last dragons.

speaker: SISU
text: All the other dragons had been turned to stone. We were drowning in a sea of Druun. Sisu sees an opening. CU on Pengu.

speaker: SISU
text: But my oldest brother, Pengu, refused to accept defeat. This is where we’d make our last stand, united. Pengudatu uses his powers to conjure a sphere of water.

speaker: SISU
text: So, one by one, they combined all their magic, creating the dragon gem. Each of the dragons concentrate and place their magic into the gem, making it stronger. With the last power added, the water-sphere solidifies into a gem. The four dragons, with their hands on the gem, turn and look at Sisu.

speaker: SISU
text: I don’t know why they chose me. It could have been any of us. All I know is I trusted them and they trusted me. And so... The four dragons hand Sisu the gem. They immediately get turned to stone by druun. Sisu stares at the gem, takes a calming breath. And on an exhale... Boom.

speaker: SISU
text: When they put their faith in me, it empowered me beyond anything I could imagine. The same can happen with Namaari.

speaker: RAYA
text: I really wish I could believe that. I once thought that we could be friends.

speaker: SISU
text: After all this, maybe you can be.

speaker: RAYA
text: Even if she wanted to help us, how could I possibly trust her?

speaker: SISU
text: But if somehow you could, you wouldn’t just bring your ba back - you’d also bring back his dream. Kumandra. Raya looks at the bridge through a crack in the wall. She plucks a flower. Off the flower, match-cut to...

speaker: RAYA
text: Do you think he would even recognize me? So much has changed.

speaker: SISU
text: Of course he will.

speaker: RAYA
text: You remind me of him.

speaker: SISU
text: Oh yeah? Strong, good-looking, with impeccable hair.

speaker: RAYA
text: Hopeful. Raya touches her father’s hand.

speaker: RAYA
text: How would I even approach Namaari after all that’s happened?

speaker: SISU
text: It may feel impossible. But sometimes you just have to take the first step even before you’re ready. Trust me. Raya nods.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay. We’ll go with your plan.

speaker: SISU
text: What? My plan? You’re gonna go with my plan?

speaker: RAYA
text: Yeah.

speaker: SISU
text: ALRIGHT! You’re not gonna regret this. But we’re gonna need a really good gift. What do you think she’s into? Cats? Knives? Cats with knives? Knives with little cats on them? An idea hits Raya.

speaker: RAYA
text: Actually, I know exactly what to give her. Raya pulls out the dragon pendant. SEQ. 380 - NAMAARI’S BURDEN

speaker: Chyron
text: Fang Virana narrates a shadow puppet show.

speaker: VIRANA
text: ... and that is how the land of Fang rose in spite of all the monsters that wanted to destroy us. Because we’re smart, resilient, and we take care of one another. Namaari storms in.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Mother, we need to talk.

speaker: KID
text: It’s Princess Namaari! All the kids cheer. Namaari smiles at the children. They melt for her.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Alright, alright. Now run along, kittens. I have to speak with the princess. The children are ushered away by their parents. As soon as the children are out of Virana’s presence, her face turns serious.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Mother, you won’t believe what I saw -

speaker: VIRANA
text: You saw a dragon. Namaari’s taken aback.

speaker: VIRANA
text: General Atitāya informed me that you’d be returning home without the gem pieces.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: It was Sisu. She can fix what we broke. She can bring everyone back. Virana overlooks the fang children playing in a nearby courtyard.

speaker: VIRANA
text: And that’s what scares me. When everyone comes back, who do you think they’ll come for? You forget, the other lands blame us for what’s happened.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: But we never meant for anyone to get hurt.

speaker: VIRANA
text: Yes, but if we had the dragon and the gem pieces, we would be forgiven. We could save the world. But more importantly, our people would remain safe.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Raya isn’t just going to give Sisu to us.

speaker: VIRANA
text: We’re not going to give her a choice.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: What are you going to do?

speaker: VIRANA
text: That’s no longer your concern, my love. You’ve done enough. Virana leaves with her General. Off Namaari, heartbroken.

speaker: TONG
text: If she refuses to help, we’ve just flushed our tactical advantage into the dung pot.

speaker: RAYA
text: I know.

speaker: BOUN
text: Yeah. She really has no reason to help us.

speaker: RAYA
text: I know.

speaker: SISU
text: This jerky is terrible.

speaker: RAYA
text: I KNOW!

speaker: TONG
text: Are you sure those four miniature menaces will be successful?

speaker: RAYA
text: I... don’t know.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Huh? The Ongis throw her a small tied up bundle and disappear. Namaari opens the bundle to find a note and... the dragon pendant.

speaker: TONG
text: There’s too much spice.

speaker: BOUN
text: Uh, no, there’s too much bamboo.

speaker: TONG
text: What do you know? You have the tastebuds of a tall baby.

speaker: BOUN
text: Well, you dress like a tall baby.

speaker: TONG
text: Give me the spoon! I’m taking over!

speaker: BOUN
text: Back off tidal wave, I’m the professional here. Raya approaches, pulls some palm sugar from her pouch.

speaker: RAYA
text: May I? Boun tastes the broth. It’s delicious.

speaker: BOUN
text: Whoa. That’s good!

speaker: RAYA
text: It’s just a little something my ba showed me.

speaker: SISU
text: Aw. Did he also show you how to make that delicious jerky?

speaker: RAYA
text: No, that was all me. It’ll be nice to share a meal with him again. Boun gives Raya a bowl of soup.

speaker: BOUN
text: I know what you mean. I have this really obnoxious sister who always tussles my hair. I can’t wait to see her the most. He hands Tong a bowl.

speaker: TONG
text: After we win the day, I look forward to filling my eyeball with the joy-tastic image of my village full again. Noi, on Tong’s shoulder, babbles - touches his face.

speaker: TONG
text: And you will be reunited with your family, Noi.

speaker: RAYA
text: Um, what did you just call her?

speaker: TONG
text: Noi. It’s her name. It’s written on her collar. Have none of you ever checked? Everyone is guilty.

speaker: TONG
text: And they think of me as the ruffian. A firework goes off in the distance and breaks the moment.

speaker: SISU
text: What’s that mean?

speaker: RAYA
text: It means we’re on. Sisu, until we get that gem and confirm Namaari’s actually on our side, promise me you’ll stay hidden. Sisu nods.

speaker: RAYA
text: I see you got my gift.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I never thought I’d see this again.

speaker: RAYA
text: Well, I tried to take good care of it. Namaari looks up at Raya. They share a smile.

speaker: RAYA
text: You’re not the only dragon nerd here. Namaari unfolds the cloth to reveal the Fang gem piece, places it on the ground, then steps back. Raya’s taken aback. It’s really here. Sisu-Dragon emerges from the woods, her eyes locked on Namaari. Raya opens her satchel, showing that she has the rest of the gems. We clock on Namaari’s eyes, noticing them.

speaker: SISU
text: The final piece.

speaker: RAYA
text: Time to bring everyone back.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Sisu and the gem pieces are coming with me. The rest of the gang emerge from the woods when they see Sisu in danger.

speaker: BOUN
text: Sisu!

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Stay back!

speaker: TONG
text: It was foolish to trust someone from Fang.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Don’t come any closer!

speaker: RAYA
text: Namaari, it doesn’t have to be like this.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I don’t have any other choice. Raya puts her hand on her sword, on guard.

speaker: SISU
text: Hey. I got this. Sisu steps forward. Namaari reactively points the crossbow at Sisu.

speaker: SISU
text: I know you don’t want to hurt anybody.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: What are you doing?

speaker: SISU
text: You just want a better world. Like we all do.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Sisu...

speaker: SISU
text: I trust you, Namaari. Raya sees her opening. She pulls her sword and tries to disarm Namaari. The crossbow goes off. An arrow cuts through the air, striking Sisu in the heart. She falls into the canal, DISSIPATING INTO THE WATER.

speaker: RAYA
text: No! Namaari, shocked, drops her crossbow and leaves with the gem piece. Raya runs towards the water.

speaker: RAYA
text: SISU!!!! Raya goes to leap into the water, but Tong stops her.

speaker: RAYA
text: No... The river begins to roil and then... Rapidly, magically, the water disappears all around them as Raya backs away from everyone...

speaker: BOUN
text: What’s happening?

speaker: TONG
text: I don’t know. It appears that with the last dragon gone so too goes the water. Now there is nothing to stop the druun. Nothing. Across the now empty riverbed, crowds of druun begin to emerge.

speaker: BOUN
text: Raya... Where’s Raya? Pan down to Raya’s empty sword sheathe and three gem pieces lying on the ground.

speaker: RAYA
text: Namaari! Namaari turns and sees Raya.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Let’s finish this, binturi. Namaari raises her weapon. Its steel catches the light. The two women rush at each other. Their blades clash.

speaker: BOUN
text: Raya! Raya doesn’t hear him, as she is completely engulfed in her battle with Namaari.

speaker: TONG
text: She cannot see us. Raya’s blinded by her own rage. Boun sees people trying to escape the city, but it’s chaos.

speaker: BOUN
text: The gem’s powers are fading. All these people are druun-food if we don’t get them out. They all nod. It’s time to be the heroes they never asked to be. Boun and Tuk-Tuk help a pair of kids hiding behind debris.

speaker: BOUN
text: Come on. Trust me, it’s gonna be okay. The kids climb onto Tuk-Tuk and Boun races them out of there.

speaker: BOUN
text: Stupid Druun! Outta my way! Go go go! As he does, Tong scoops up as many people as possible to get them out of the city.

speaker: TONG
text: Up we go. Druun start to close in on a scared civilian couple. With the help of the Ongis, Noi uses a gem shard to ward it away.

speaker: BOUN
text: Get to the water! Keep going! Keep going!

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I never meant for any of this to happen.

speaker: RAYA
text: Liar!

speaker: NAMAARI
text: I don’t care if you believe me. Sisu did. But you didn’t trust her. That’s why we’re here. Raya hesitates.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: Do whatever you want, but you’re as much to blame for Sisu’s death as I am. Raya looks at the reflection of her eyes in her raised blade. Namaari closes her eyes. Raya looks back at her own reflection in her sword and notices the rage in her eyes. Her ears catch the sound of her friends helping people amid the destruction. She looks outside and sees...

speaker: BOUN
text: Tong, there’s still people back there! Hurry, we’re running out of time.

speaker: TONG
text: Got it! Everyone out! Come with me! Focus on Namaari. She looks over to where Raya once stood and discovers... Raya’s gone.

speaker: RAYA
text: Okay, Tuk. These are the last of them. Go! We’re right behind you. Suddenly... A druun steps in between Tuk-Tuk and the exit.

speaker: RAYA
text: Tuk-Tuk! The kids and Tuk-Tuk are in jeopardy until... Namaari leaps in front of the Druun and scares it away with her own dragon gem shard.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: What are you waiting for? Go! Tuk-Tuk rolls away. Namaari and Raya share a look. But then - Another earthquake hits. Stones fall and crash. All exits are blocked.

speaker: BOUN
text: Raya!

speaker: TONG
text: It’s the one who slayed Sisu! Another quake. The team falls into rubble.

speaker: BOUN
text: Raya! Raya! Raya sees her team, they’re completely engulfed by druun. Their gems are holding them off, but the druun continue to advance as the gem’s powers fade.

speaker: BOUN
text: The gem’s magic is almost gone!

speaker: TONG
text: They aren’t backing off!

speaker: BOUN
text: They’re everywhere! Raya’s eyes catch something shiny on the ground, it’s... the dragon pendent. She picks it up, triggering a memory. FLASHBACK. She’s momentarily transported back to the Heart temple where the druun once swirled around Sisu and her siblings. She hears Sisu’s faint voice from the past...

speaker: SISU
text: I don’t know why they chose me. It could have been any one of us. All I know is I trusted them and they trusted me. And so...

speaker: RAYA
text: Everyone, give me your gems! We can still put it together, it can still work!

speaker: BOUN
text: Sisu’s gone, Raya. We don’t have her magic.

speaker: RAYA
text: It’s not about her magic. It’s about trust.

speaker: NAMAARI
text: WHAT?

speaker: RAYA
text: That’s why it worked. That’s why we can do it too. By doing the one thing Sisu wanted us to do — what my Ba wanted us to do — to finally trust each other and fix this. But we have to come together. Please. Raya looks to her friends -

speaker: TONG
text: After what she’s done -

speaker: BOUN
text: We’ll never trust her! Noi hisses! Raya looks at her divided team and understands what she must do.

speaker: RAYA
text: Then let me take the first step. Raya hands Namaari her gem piece.

speaker: BOUN
text: Raya, no! Raya steps away and is immediately turned to stone. The group is in shock. Did that just really happen? Namaari stares at the gem piece in her hands. She looks up and sees Boun, his eyes locked onto Raya and his friends. Moved by what he just saw, he knows what he has to do. He hands his gem to Namaari as well... and then goes to Raya, taking her hand, and being turned to stone as well. Namaari’s shook. But to add to her confusion, Noi and Tong follow suit. They give up their gem pieces to her and moments later, they too are turned to stone along with the Ongis. Pan over to Namaari. She has all the gems. She’s in shock... She looks up and sees... an exit. She has a choice... Namaari starts to flee, but stops and looks at Raya’s stone face... It’s too much, she can’t keep going. Namaari returns and hurriedly assembles the gem pieces as the pulsing light within them continues to DIM... They stick together, but the pulsing light within is growing

speaker: BOUN
text: It worked! It worked! It -- Boun trips, looks at his feet which are still turning back to normal. Noi and the Ongis jump happily on Tong. Finally... Raya looks over to find Namaari’s hand touching her shoulder. Raya’s trust in her indeed transformed her. Raya touches Namaari’s hand, they acknowledge something new has been born between them.

speaker: RAYA
text: Tuk-Tuk! The two friends reunite as... Water once again flows abundantly all around them - the dragon river has returned.

speaker: SISU
text: Pranee! Amba! Jagan! Pengu! Sisu runs on the raindrops alongside her siblings, then lowers down to the ground. Raya runs to her friend as Sisu playfully splashes her.

speaker: SISU
text: Raya!

speaker: RAYA
text: Sisu.

speaker: SISU
text: I. Am. So hungry.

speaker: RAYA
text: I got some jerky.

speaker: SISU
text: Not that hungry. They smile and hug. Tong shoves Noi in Sisu’s face.

speaker: NOI
text: Soo-soo!

speaker: TONG
text: Ah! It is good to breathe in your glorious dragon stench again.

speaker: SISU
text: Okay, I take that as a compliment. Namaari stands apart, chastened. She remorsefully bows to Sisu who... ... reaches out and pulls Namaari into the group. She hugs them all.

speaker: RAYA
text: Ba. ... her father standing there, cleaning up debris. He turns to catch her eyes.

speaker: BENJA
text: Dewdrop? Raya breaks into a run and they hug tightly, tears running down her face. Benja gives his daughter a kiss on the cheek and then sees above him... SISU standing on the bridge before him. Benja is gobsmacked. He bows in respect to the dragon.

speaker: BENJA
text: Is that... really her? Sisu smiles.

speaker: SISU
text: Chief Benja, your daughter did you proud. Hope you don’t mind. She brought some friends. Sisu steps aside to reveal Boun, Tong, Noi, the Ongis, and Namaari standing side-by-side. Behind them, hundreds of citizens from all over Kumandra are lined up. Benja is overwhelmed, he can’t believe his eyes.

speaker: RAYA
text: Ba, welcome to... Kumandra. As the crowd moves in to cross the bridge, Benja looks at Raya - this is more than anything he could have dreamed of. As Benja and Raya walk into Heart with the crowd, he puts an arm around her and she leans her head on his shoulder. Sisu flies through the air one last time past Raya, who smiles at her from below.
